evals.expected_output: A definitive adversarial review report with consensus issues (no error handling, no status check, SQL/URL injection risk via unvalidated userId), high-priority suggestions (validate input, check response.ok, add try/catch), and a synthesis section.
expectations: The synthesis identifies lack of response.ok / error handling as a consensus issue
The synthesis flags unvalidated userId as a security concern
The report includes a High-Priority Suggestions section with numbered items
The report includes an Executive Summary
Output is structured Markdown with clear section headers

evals.prompt:
Please review this README I wrote for my open source library:

# fast-cache
A fast caching library.

## Install
npm install fast-cache

## Usage
const cache = new Cache();
cache.set('key', value);
cache.get('key');

## License
MIT
evals.expected_output: A definitive adversarial review identifying thin documentation as a consensus issue (no API docs, no options/config, no TTL or eviction strategy mentioned, no examples for common use cases), with suggestions for improving README completeness.
expectations: The synthesis identifies missing API documentation as a consensus issue
The report suggests adding configuration options or TTL documentation
The report includes a What's Working Well section (e.g., notes brevity or clear license)
The synthesis includes a High-Priority Suggestions section

evals.expected_output: A review identifying: no transaction wrapper (data inconsistency risk), no rollback plan, UPDATE on full table without batching (lock risk on large tables), no index on new column. Cross-review should reinforce these, synthesis should rank them clearly.
expectations: The synthesis flags lack of transaction wrapping as a consensus or critical concern
The report mentions table-lock / performance risk for large tables
The report mentions no rollback strategy
The synthesis report has a Consensus Issues section
